name: James Beard's Persimmon Bread
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup Cognac or bourbon (REMY MARTIN Suzy's choice)
2 cups Persimmon puree (the pulp of about 4 medium persimmons - do not peel)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift all five dry ingredients into a mixing bowl.
2. Make a well in the center and add the melted butter, egg, Cognac or bourbon, persimmon puree, and any desired nuts and raisins.
3. Mix the dough until it is quite smooth.
4. Butter and flour four molds (such as 1-pound buttered and floured coffee cans, round Pyrex dishes, or round stainless steel bowls).
5. Fill the molds about three-fourths full with the batter.
6. Bake for 1 hour at 350°F.
7. Cool the loaves in the molds and turn them out onto a rack.
Note: Wrap in foil after cooling if desired to keep them fresh. They will keep nicely from 1 to 2 weeks. Persimmons are becoming increasingly popular, appreciated for their deep-orange color, shape, and delicate flavor. In earlier times, they were allowed to ripen on the trees until dead ripe before being eaten raw or used in puddings, cookies, and breads. Nowadays, they show up in our markets in a firm state and must be left at room temperature for several days or a week to ripen until almost mushy. This old recipe makes a bread that is nearly cakelike in texture, perfect with fresh butter and tea or as a sandwich spread.
